source: CEDR- Contextualized Embeddings for Document Ranking

concepts.finding: CEDR integrates BERT with existing neural ranking models (KNRM, Conv-KNRM, PACRR). Instead of using BERT alone, uses BERT to generate contextualized embeddings for query and document terms, then passes to the ranking model. BERT + KNRM achieves best results: 5-10% NDCG improvement over non-contextualized baselines. Ablation shows both BERT representations and ranking model architecture matter.
concepts.implication: For keyword-intent matching, contextualized embeddings from a language model can enrich keyword representations before passing to a matching/ranking model. BERT provides disambiguation (e.g., apple as fruit vs brand).
concepts.open_question: Can keyword embeddings from BERT (or similar PLMs) capture intent as well as embeddings from co-occurrence graphs? Which PLM layer provides the best keyword representations for intent?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: CEDR-style integration of contextualized PLM embeddings with intent matching functions outperforms either PLM-only or co-occurrence-only keyword embeddings for intent assignment.
